---
title: forensicface
---


::: {#cell-1 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from forensicface.app import ForensicFace
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
```
:::



> A Python package for forensic face examination


## Installation

Python >= 3.13 is required.  

An Nvidia GPU with CUDA properly configured is recommended, but not required. It is 
automatically detected and used if available.   

Installing forensicface on a dedicated Python virtual environment is highly recommended.  

It works on Windows and Linux. It should work on MacOS, but it has naver been tested.  

You can install forensicface via pip:
```sh
pip install forensicface
```

After installing forensicface, you'll need to manually put the model files in the folder `~/.forensicface/models/`  
If you don't have these files, send an e-mail to `rafael.ror <at> pf.gov.br`  

## Basic usage

::: {#cell-7 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from forensicface.app import ForensicFace

ff = ForensicFace()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[ForensicFace] Initialized with configuration:
                  loaded_models=['sepaelv2']
                  modules=['detection', 'headpose', 'genderage', 'cr_fiqa']
                  det_size=(320, 320)
                  session_providers=all models use CUDAExecutionProvider

```
:::
:::


Processing an image file with a single face. forensicface returns a dictionary with the following keys:  
- 'ipd' - interpuppilary distance
- 'fiqa_score' - a facial image quality score
- 'gender' - estimated sex, either M or F
- 'age' - estimated age, in years
- 'yaw', 'pitch' and 'roll'  - estimated head pose angles
- 'det_score' - face detection score
- 'keypoints' - x and y coordinates of the eyes, tip of the nose and corners of the mouth.
- 'bbox' - bounding box of the face
- 'embedding' - vector representation of the face
- 'aligned_face' - RGB image of the aligned face, with 112 x 112 pixels. This is the input to the model that extracts the embedding.

::: {#cell-9 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
results = ff.process_image("obama2.png")
results.keys()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
FutureWarning: process_image: The return of this function when 'single_face = True' will change in a future release.
Instead of returning a dict, it will return a list (with one dict). 
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3}
```
dict_keys(['ipd', 'fiqa_score', 'gender', 'age', 'yaw', 'pitch', 'roll', 'det_score', 'keypoints', 'bbox', 'embedding', 'aligned_face'])
```
:::
:::


::: {#cell-10 .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
results.get("gender"), results.get("age"), results.get("yaw")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4}
```
('M', 60, np.float32(-37.767048))
```
:::
:::


It's possible to have the keypoints drawn on the aligned face. This is useful to check if the face detection and alignment were correct.

::: {#cell-12 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
aligned_img_with_kps = ff.process_image("obama2.png", draw_keypoints=True).get(
    "aligned_face"
)
plt.imshow(aligned_img_with_kps)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
FutureWarning: process_image: The return of this function when 'single_face = True' will change in a future release.
Instead of returning a dict, it will return a list (with one dict). 
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/cell-6-output-2.png){}
:::
:::


Processing an image file with multiple faces. forensicface returns a list of dictionaries, with the same keys as in the single face case.

## Processamento básico de imagens
Obter pontos de referência, distância interpupilar, representação vetorial, a face alinhada com dimensão fixa (112x112), estimativas de sexo, idade, pose (_pitch_, _yaw_, _roll_) e qualidade. Opcionalmente, é possível anotar a face alinhada com os pontos de referência utilizados no alinhamento (parâmetro `draw_kypoints`).

::: {#cell-15 .cell execution_count=6}
``` {.python .cell-code}
results = ff.process_image_single_face("obama2.png", draw_keypoints=True)
results.keys()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6}
```
dict_keys(['ipd', 'fiqa_score', 'gender', 'age', 'yaw', 'pitch', 'roll', 'det_score', 'keypoints', 'bbox', 'embedding', 'aligned_face'])
```
:::
:::


::: {#cell-16 .cell execution_count=7}
``` {.python .cell-code}
plt.imshow(results["aligned_face"])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/cell-8-output-1.png){}
:::
:::


Comparar duas imagens faciais e obter o escore de similaridade.

::: {#cell-18 .cell execution_count=8}
``` {.python .cell-code}
ff.compare("obama.png", "obama2.png")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=8}
```
np.float32(0.85561067)
```
:::
:::


Agregar embeddings de duas imagens faciais em uma única representação, com ponderação por qualidade

::: {#cell-20 .cell execution_count=9}
``` {.python .cell-code}
agg = ff.aggregate_from_images(["obama.png", "obama2.png"], quality_weight=True)
agg.shape
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9}
```
(512,)
```
:::
:::


## Estimativa de qualidade CR-FIQA

Estimativa de qualidade pelo método [CR-FIQA](https://github.com/fdbtrs/CR-FIQA)

Para desabilitar, instancie o forensicface com a opção extended = False:

``ff = ForensicFace(extended=False)``

Obs.: a opção `extended = False` também desabilita as estimativas de sexo, idade e pose.

::: {#cell-22 .cell execution_count=10}
``` {.python .cell-code}
good = ff.process_image("001_frontal.jpg")
bad = ff.process_image("001_cam1_1.jpg")
good["fiqa_score"], bad["fiqa_score"]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=10}
```
(np.float32(2.3785863), np.float32(1.4386019))
```
:::
:::


## Crédito dos modelos utilizados

- Detecção, gênero (M/F), idade e pose (pitch, yaw, roll): [insightface](https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface)

- Reconhecimento: [adaface](https://github.com/mk-minchul/AdaFace)

- Estimativa de qualidade: [CR-FIQA](https://github.com/fdbtrs/CR-FIQA)
